nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project documentation explaining the autonomous AI research experiment concept, quick start guide, and design choices for the autoresearch framework

nodes.name: prepare.py
nodes.summary: One-time data preparation script that downloads training data shards, trains a BPE tokenizer, and provides runtime utilities for data loading and model evaluation

children.name: download_single_shard
children.summary: Downloads one parquet shard from HuggingFace with retry logic and temporary file handling

children.name: download_data
children.summary: Downloads training shards and the pinned validation shard in parallel using multiprocessing

children.name: list_parquet_files
children.summary: Returns a sorted list of parquet file paths in the data directory

children.name: text_iterator
children.summary: Yields documents from training parquet files with optional char and document limits

children.name: train_tokenizer
children.summary: Trains a BPE tokenizer using rustbpe and saves it as a tiktoken pickle with token_bytes lookup for BPB evaluation

children.name: Tokenizer
children.summary: Minimal tokenizer wrapper around tiktoken encoding with BOS token support

children.name: __init__
children.summary: Initializes the tokenizer wrapper and stores the BOS token ID

children.name: from_directory
children.summary: Class method that loads a trained tokenizer from the tokenizer directory

children.name: get_vocab_size
children.summary: Returns the vocabulary size of the tokenizer

children.name: get_bos_token_id
children.summary: Returns the BOS (beginning of sequence) token ID

children.name: encode
children.summary: Encodes text or batch of texts to token IDs with optional BOS prepending

children.name: decode
children.summary: Decodes token IDs back to text

children.name: get_token_bytes
children.summary: Loads and returns the token_bytes tensor used for BPB evaluation

children.name: _document_batches
children.summary: Private infinite iterator that yields document batches from parquet files for the data loader

children.name: make_dataloader
children.summary: Creates a BOS-aligned dataloader with best-fit packing for 100% utilization without padding
children.signature: def make_dataloader(tokenizer, B, T, split, buffer_size=1000)

children.name: evaluate_bpb
children.summary: Evaluates model performance using bits per byte (BPB) metric, which is vocab-size-independent and uses fixed evaluation tokens

nodes.name: program.md
nodes.summary: AI agent instructions document that defines the autonomous research workflow, experiment loop, logging format, and constraints for modifying only train.py

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Project configuration file defining dependencies, Python version requirements, and PyTorch index source for CUDA 12.8

nodes.name: autoresearch/train.py
nodes.summary: Main training script for Autoresearch GPT model pretraining with single-GPU optimization and custom MuonAdamW optimizer.

children.name: GPTConfig
children.summary: Configuration dataclass for GPT model parameters.

children.name: norm
children.summary: Applies RMS normalization to input tensor.

children.name: has_ve
children.summary: Determines if a layer should have value embedding based on alternating pattern.

children.name: apply_rotary_emb
children.summary: Applies rotary positional embeddings to query and key tensors.

children.name: CausalSelfAttention
children.summary: Causal self-attention layer with value embedding and flash attention.

children.name: __init__
children.summary: Initializes attention layer with linear projections and value embedding gate.

children.name: forward
children.summary: Forward pass with value embedding, rotary embeddings, and flash attention.
children.signature: def forward(self, x, ve, cos_sin, window_size):

children.name: MLP
children.summary: Feed-forward MLP layer with ReLU squared activation.

children.name: __init__
children.summary: Initializes MLP with linear layers.

children.name: forward
children.summary: Forward pass with ReLU squared activation.

children.name: Block
children.summary: Transformer block combining attention and MLP with residual connections.

children.name: __init__
children.summary: Initializes block with attention and MLP components.

children.name: forward
children.summary: Forward pass with residual connections and normalization.
children.signature: def forward(self, x, ve, cos_sin, window_size):

children.name: GPT
children.summary: Main GPT model with transformer architecture and custom optimizations.

children.name: __init__
children.summary: Initializes GPT model with embeddings, transformer blocks, and parameters.

children.name: init_weights
children.summary: Initializes model weights with specific scaling and distributions.

children.name: _precompute_rotary_embeddings
children.summary: Precomputes rotary positional embeddings for attention layers.

children.name: _compute_window_sizes
children.summary: Computes sliding window sizes for attention based on pattern.

children.name: estimate_flops
children.summary: Estimates FLOPs per token for forward and backward passes.

children.name: num_scaling_params
children.summary: Counts parameters by category for scaling analysis.

children.name: setup_optimizer
children.summary: Sets up MuonAdamW optimizer with parameter groups and learning rates.
children.signature: def setup_optimizer(self, unembedding_lr=0.004, embedding_lr=0.2, matrix_lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.0, adam_betas=(0.8, 0.95), scalar_lr=0.5):

children.name: forward
children.summary: Forward pass through transformer with value embeddings and softcap.

children.name: adamw_step_fused
children.summary: Fused AdamW optimization step with torch.compile for performance.
children.signature: @torch.compile(dynamic=False, fullgraph=True) def adamw_step_fused(p, grad, exp_avg, exp_avg_sq, step_t, lr_t, beta1_t, beta2_t, eps_t, wd_t):

children.name: muon_step_fused
children.summary: Fused Muon optimization step with polar express orthogonalization.
children.signature: @torch.compile(dynamic=False, fullgraph=True) def muon_step_fused(stacked_grads, stacked_params, momentum_buffer, second_momentum_buffer, momentum_t, lr_t, wd_t, beta2_t, ns_steps, red_dim):

children.name: MuonAdamW
children.summary: Combined optimizer using Muon for matrices and AdamW for other parameters.

children.name: __init__
children.summary: Initializes optimizer with parameter groups and CPU tensors.

children.name: _step_adamw
children.summary: Performs AdamW optimization step for non-matrix parameters.

children.name: _step_muon
children.summary: Performs Muon optimization step for matrix parameters.

children.name: step
children.summary: Main optimization step that dispatches to appropriate method.

children.name: build_model_config
children.summary: Builds GPT configuration based on depth and aspect ratio.

children.name: get_lr_multiplier
children.summary: Computes learning rate multiplier based on training progress.

children.name: get_muon_momentum
children.summary: Computes Muon momentum based on training step.

children.name: get_weight_decay
children.summary: Computes weight decay based on training progress.

tours.name: Understanding the Project
tours.description: Learn what this autonomous AI research experiment is about and how it works

steps.narrative: Start here to understand the project's purpose: an autonomous AI agent that performs pretraining experiments. The README explains the core concept, quick start guide, and design philosophy.
This document defines the AI agent's behavior—its research loop, logging format, and constraints. It's the 'brain' that decides what experiments to run and how to measure results.
The project configuration file shows dependencies and Python version requirements. Note the PyTorch CUDA 12.8 index—this project is optimized for GPU training.
The core training script that the agent iteratively modifies. Contains the Autoresearch GPT model, custom MuonAdamW optimizer, and single-GPU pretraining logic.

tours.name: Training Pipeline Flow
tours.description: Follow the data flow from preparation through model training

steps.narrative: First, ensure dependencies are installed. This configuration sets up the environment with PyTorch and CUDA support needed for heavy computation.
Run this one-time script to download data shards and train a BPE tokenizer. It also provides utilities for data loading that the training script will import.
The training script imports utilities from prepare.py and runs the actual pretraining. This is where the model learns from the prepared data.

tours.name: Quick Start Guide
tours.description: Get up and running with the minimal steps needed to begin

steps.narrative: Read the documentation to verify your environment meets requirements and understand what you're about to run. Contains the essential setup commands.
Install dependencies using this configuration. This ensures you have the correct versions of all packages, especially the CUDA-optimized PyTorch.
Execute this script once to prepare your data. It downloads training shards and builds the tokenizer—both prerequisites before any training can begin.
Now you're ready to train. This script runs the pretraining loop; for the autonomous mode, the AI agent will iteratively modify and re-run this file.
